fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Carolina Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to South Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity south carolina, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to south carolina, pharmacy technician compact south carolina, south carolina pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: South Carolina Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: South Carolina Department of Labor, Licensing and Regulation (LLR)
board.mailing_address: South Carolina Board of Pharmacy, 110 Centerview Dr, PO Box 11921, Columbia, SC 29211-1921

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in South Carolina.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you are at least 16 years of age and possess a high school diploma or GED, or are currently enrolled in high school. These are fundamental requirements for registration in South Carolina.

steps.title: Initiate Online Application
steps.description: Navigate to the SC LLR eService Online Application Portal at eservice.llr.sc.gov/OnlineApplication/. New users will need to create an account by registering with an email address. After logging in, select 'Apply for a New License/Registration' and then locate and choose the 'Pharmacy Technician' application type to begin.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: You are required to undergo a fingerprint-based criminal background check through IdentoGO. Follow the specific instructions provided within the online application portal or the Pharmacy Technician Application Packet (llr.sc.gov/bop/forms/technician/Pharmacy%20Technician%20Application.pdf) to schedule your fingerprints using the correct service code for the SC Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fee
steps.description: Ensure all sections of your online application are thoroughly completed and any required documents are uploaded. Pay the non-refundable application fee of $50 directly through the SC LLR eService Online Application Portal.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Notification
steps.description: After submitting your application and background check, the South Carolina Board of Pharmacy will review your credentials. You will be notified of your registration status once your application has been processed and approved.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: South Carolina offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the South Carolina Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in South Carolina by submitting a new application to the South Carolina Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any South Carolina-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a South Carolina pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does South Carolina have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in South Carolina. To work in South Carolina, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to South Carolina?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in South Carolina by submitting a new application to the South Carolina Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any South Carolina-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 to 4 Weeks.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in South Carolina
hero.dek: Apply online for a Pharmacy Technician license through the SC LLR eService Online Application Portal (eservice.llr.sc.gov/OnlineApplication/). New users must register for an account, log in, and select 'Apply for a New License/Registration' to find the Pharmacy Technician application.
